title: Is there a reason we are hard coding the storage driver type in the database.
body: I would want to be able to use multiple different storages in podman at the same time.  But the database refuses to allow me?

comments.body: @mheon WHY?
I am running podman in my homedir, and I want to play with fuse-overlay, but I have to blow away all containers, since i ran originally with the vfs driver.

If you swap storage driver type with active containers, suddenly the
contents of your storage dirs become useless, and we lose whatever active
containers you have - you need to delete them and recreate them on the new
storage driver. Basically, we're trying to ensure database consistency by
making sure everyone using it can see and use the same set of containers in
c/storage

On Sat, Nov 17, 2018, 06:43 Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com wrote:

> I am running podman in my homedir, and I want to play with fuse-overlay,
> but I have to blow away all containers, since i ran originally with the vfs
> driver.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1826#issuecomment-439610578>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AHYHCGTebqPvj1x06Hv-UM_BIRf0DFTCks5uv_ZsgaJpZM4YnaOb>
> .
>

I think our basic answer for swapping to fuse-overlayfs is tell people to
blow away their database after saving all relevant containers...

On Sat, Nov 17, 2018, 09:48 Matthew Heon <matthew.heon@gmail.com wrote:

> If you swap storage driver type with active containers, suddenly the
> contents of your storage dirs become useless, and we lose whatever active
> containers you have - you need to delete them and recreate them on the new
> storage driver. Basically, we're trying to ensure database consistency by
> making sure everyone using it can see and use the same set of containers in
> c/storage
>
> On Sat, Nov 17, 2018, 06:43 Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com
> wrote:
>
>> I am running podman in my homedir, and I want to play with fuse-overlay,
>> but I have to blow away all containers, since i ran originally with the vfs
>> driver.
>>
>> —
>> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
>> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
>> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1826#issuecomment-439610578>,
>> or mute the thread
>> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AHYHCGTebqPvj1x06Hv-UM_BIRf0DFTCks5uv_ZsgaJpZM4YnaOb>
>> .
>>
>

Except that figuring out where your content is in your homedirs is difficult.  And podman info stops working.  Perhaps we could make the error indicate to them that changing your storage driver requires a reset of storage.

But I still would like to run with two different storages at the same time.

What if I want to experiment with KATA containers or use kata for certain workloads while I use runc for others.  KATA works much better with devicemapper, while I definitely want to use overlay with traditional containers.   Is there any reason we can not support this use case?

> [...] Perhaps we could make the error indicate to them that changing your storage driver requires a reset of storage.

Would changing the location of the storage work as well? If so, couldn't storage take care of that on its own and create per-driver databases?

Well if we moved the libpod directory under the storage-driver directory it could work.
@mheon Would this cause large issues?

We'd end up with the problem where we lost all the user's containers on driver change, but that can probably be fixed by better documenting behavior.

I don't know how well storage would appreciate this - some of the storage drivers (including, I think, overlay?) could be doing some things with mount on the driver directory that might make some libpod features break?

Well we could allways create overlay-libpod.

And yes I expect the containers to disappear when the drive is changed.

I completely agree with Dan here. I'm trying to move from one driver to another right now and can't:
```
Could not get runtime: database graph driver name overlay does not match our graph driver name vfs: database configuration mismatch
```
If you care about running containers, you could easily print a warning that there are containers running with driver $x while driver $y is being used right now.

I think our problem with the proposed fix is that it's a breaking change
for everyone using Podman. Everyone, rootless and not, will lose all their
containers of we move the DB. We need a way of maintaining backwards
compatibility if we want to fix this.

On Fri, Dec 7, 2018, 04:54 Tomas Tomecek <notifications@github.com wrote:

> I completely agree with Dan here. I'm trying to move from one driver to

I agree, I was thinking that the tool shold look to see if the old format exists and  matches the driver, then we copy/mv it to the new directory.  I will attempt to do this on the next pass.
where are we on this?
@rhatdan was working on a PR to move the DB inside the storage driver-specific directories to allow use of multiple drivers. Unfortunately that's a breaking change, so I think it's stalled

https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1847
For some reason I can not get it to pass all tests...
I would still like to get this PR in.  But I have not figured out why rootless does not like it. Hoping that @giuseppe takes it over.
This PR Finally passes tests, but now need to work on the upgrade path.  If anyone wants to grab it, you are welcome to take it.
do we still need this open?
I have had a PR for this open for many many months.  Post 1.5, I would like to finally get it merged.
@rhatdan did you get your pr merged on this so we can close it ?
Nope.
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
PR Is still out there.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
PR is still out there.
